run_summary: Screen 1 holds 4 positions (all 3–5 days old, mixed P&L) across two distinct catalyst triggers. One new flag (DUOL) arrives on Gemini image-gen; threat is indirect and panic is unjustified, but filings evidence is thin and confidence is exactly at threshold — WATCH rather than BUY to avoid overcommitting on an edtech read with limited hard evidence.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Gemini's personalized AI image generation now free for US users
entries.thesis: Today's image-gen announcement is functionally irrelevant to Duolingo's product (text/audio-driven habit loop with gamification). Retail reflexively sells edtech on any frontier-AI release, but Gemini personalized image generation is not the LLM-tutor threat that would actually erode DUOL's TAM. The moat is the dopamine/streak loop, not content uniqueness, and DUOL is also already extended down on the week — sympathy compounding rather than fundamentals.
entries.what_kills: Google announces a Gemini-powered language tutor directly competing with DUOL; DAU growth visibly decelerates in the next quarterly print; Duolingo Max subscriber metrics disappoint.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3 (at min threshold), panic_calibration unjustified, threat_assessment is 'indirect' (not 'minimal' or 'none'). Filings evidence exists (Risk Factors mention GenAI broadly + translation-tool risk) but is generic and not specific to Gemini image generation. The real threat to DUOL is LLM-powered language tutors, not image models — thesis logic is sound. However, the sympathy-fade read lacks the sharp clarity required for BUY: threat_assessment is indirect (some non-zero exposure), confidence is exactly at threshold, and filings tie to broad GenAI risk rather than concrete product threat. WATCH allows repricing to develop over next 2–3 trading days; if DUOL stabilizes and no Google tutor announcement surfaces, revisit for EXPLORATORY BUY.
